Question: I went to Virtue restaurant & bar in Chicago for my birthday on March 22, 2021 and the main course I had was delicious!  Unfortunately, when I went back about a month later on April 21, it was no longer on the dinner menu.  Using the Wayback Machine, can you help me figure out which main course was on the dinner menu for Virtue on March 22, 2021 but not April 21, 2021? Answer using the singular form, without articles.

Annotator Metadata.Steps:
1. Search the web for "Virtue restaurant & bar Chicago"
2. Find the restaurant's website, https://www.virtuerestaurant.com
3. Find the page for the dinner menu, https://www.virtuerestaurant.com/menus/
4. Paste the URL of this page into the Wayback Machine at web.archive.org
5. Open the versions of the page archived on March 22, 2021 and April 21, 2021
6. Ensure that both pages are open to the "dinner menu" tab
7. Find the "large ration" that was present on the March 22 version of the menu but not April 21: shrimp
Annotator Metadata.Tools:
1. Web browser
2. Search engine
3. Access to the Internet Archive, web.archive.org
4. Text processing/diff tool

Question:
It is 1999. Before you party like it is 1999, please assist me in settling a bet.

Fiona Apple and Paula Cole released albums prior to 1999. Of these albums, which didn't receive a letter grade from Robert Christgau? Provide your answer as a comma delimited list of album titles, sorted alphabetically.

Annotator Metadata.Steps:
1. search "Fiona Apple discography"
2. find her album released prior to 1999 was "Tidal"
3. search "Paula Cole discography"
4. find her album released prior to 1999 was "This Fire" and "Harbinger".
5. search "Robert Christgau"
6. use his website to search "Fiona Apple"
7. note his review for Tidal was an emoticon, not a letter grade
8. use his website to search "Paula Cole"
9. note his review for This Fire was a C+ and that he did not review Harbinger.

Question: In the 2018 VSCode blog post on replit.com, what was the command they clicked on in the last video to remove extra lines?

Annotator Metadata.Steps:
1. Opened replit.com.
2. Clicked "Blog".
3. Searched "vscode".
4. Opened "Zero Setup VSCode Intelligence" from 2018.
5. Scrolled down to the bottom video.
6. Noted the command used (Format Document).
